case_title: FIROUZ MAHDAVI VS. OSMAN KHAN et al

docket_entries.description: POS OF X-COMPLAINT FILED BY CROSS COMPLAINANT KHAN, OSMAN
DECLARATION OF SIERRA ARBALLO ISO OPPOS FILED BY DEFENDANT KHAN, OSMAN
CASE MANAGEMENT STATEMENT FILED BY PLAINTIFF MAHDAVI, FIROUZ JURY DEMANDED, ESTIMATED TIME FOR TRIAL: 4.0 DAYS
SUMMONS ON COMPLAINT, PROOF OF SERVICE ONLY, FILED BY PLAINTIFF MAHDAVI, FIROUZ SERVED JAN-26-2014, PERSONAL SERVICE AS TO DEFENDANT WILSON, VINCENT
DEMAND FOR JURY FILED BY DEFENDANT WILSON, VINCENT

LAW AND MOTION 302, CROSS DEFENDANT VINCENT WILSON'S DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED. A CROSS-COMPLAINT MAY INCORPORATE BY REFERENCE ALLEGATIONS CONTAINED IN THE UNDERLYING COMPLAINT. (PINE TERRACE APARTMENTS, L.P. V. WINDSCAPE, LLC (2009) 170 CAL.APP.4TH 1, 19; SEE ALSO WEIL & BROWN, CAL. PRACTICE GUIDE: CIV. PROC. BEFORE TRIAL (THE RUTTER GROUP 2013) 6:239, "CROSS-COMPLAINTS FOR INDEMNIFICATION USUALLY INCORPORATE BY REFERENCE THE ALLEGATIONS OF THE COMPLAINT AS A 'SHORTHAND' WAY OF SHOWING THE EXISTENCE OF THE CLAIM FOR WHICH INDEMNIFICATION IS SOUGHT.") THE MAJORITY OF THE AUTHORITIES CITED BY CROSS-DEFENDANT DO NOT ADDRESS INCORPORATION BY REFERENCE. THE ONE CASE CITED BY CROSS-DEFENDANT THAT ADDRESSES INCORPORATION BY REFERENCES STATES THAT "A CROSS-COMPLAINT MUST ITSELF BE SUFFICIENT AND CANNOT BE CURED BY THE PLEADINGS IN THE ORIGINAL ACTION, THAT IS THE COMPLAINT AND ANSWER, APART FROM PERMISSIBLE INCORPORATION BY REFERENCE." (BERG V. INVESTORS REAL ESTATE LOAN CO. (1962) 207 CAL.APP.2D 808, 816.) THE CROSS-COMPLAINT STATES A CAUSE OF ACTION, AS THE UNDERLYING COMPLAINT ALLEGES THAT BOTH CROSS-COMPLAINANT AND CROSS-DEFENDANT CONTRIBUTED TO PLAINTIFF'S INJURIES. THE COURT DID NOT CONSIDER THE DECLARATIONS FILED IN SUPPORT OF AND IN OPPOSITION TO THE DEMURRER. (SEE BLANK V. KIRWAN (1985) 39 CAL.3D 311, 318.) (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER.) JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; REPORTER: CAROL KAREN CSR# 8189.

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Dec-26-2014 Vacated

MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-05-2014 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-11-2014 Set For Trial On Dec-29-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-03-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
David M. Trent, Esq. of Lauria Tokunaga Gates & Linn LLP, Tel. 916.492.2000, for cross defendant and moving party;
Sierra Arballo, Esq. of Philip M. Andersen & Associates, Tel. 925.225.6838, for opposing party, via Court Call.

RULING: The matter was argued. After argument, the Court adopted the tentative ruling. CROSS DEFENDANT VINCENT WILSON'S DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT is overruled. A cross-complaint may incorporate by reference allegations contained in the underlying complaint. (Pine Terrace Apartments, L.P. v. Windscape, LLC (2009) 170 Cal.App.4th 1, 19; see also Weil & Brown, Cal. Practice Guide: Civ. Proc. Before Trial (The Rutter Group 2013) 6:239, "Cross-complaints for indemnification usually incorporate by reference the allegations of the complaint as a 'shorthand' way of showing the existence of the claim for which indemnification is sought.") The majority of the authorities cited by Cross-Defendant do not address incorporation by reference. The one case cited by Cross-Defendant that addresses incorporation by references states that "a cross-complaint must itself be sufficient and cannot be cured by the pleadings in the original action, that is the complaint and answer, apart from permissible incorporation by reference." (Berg v. Investors Real Estate Loan Co. (1962) 207 Cal.App.2d 808, 816.) The Cross-Complaint states a cause of action, as the underlying complaint alleges that both Cross-Complainant and Cross-Defendant contributed to plaintiff's injuries. The Court did not consider the declarations filed in support of and in opposition to the demurrer. (See Blank v. Kirwan (1985) 39 Cal.3d 311, 318.) (Prevailing party to prepare a proposed order.) Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Carol Karen CSR# 8189.
